title: Yashwantgad Fort
tagline: Yashwantgad Fort — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: Yashwantgad Fort is located in Redi, Maharashtra, near the Maharashtra-Goa border.
overview.description: Yashwantgad Fort is located in Redi, Maharashtra, near the Maharashtra-Goa border. It sits on a small hill north of Terekhol Creek. The fort, now a tree-entangled ruin, overlooks the beaches of the southern Maharashtra coast.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Ganapati Temple, Redi
topPlaces.description: The Ganapati temple of Redi is located approximately 30 km from Vengurla, in the small village of Redi, Maharashtra, India. The town in which this temple is situated contains iron ore mines and the Ganpati (Ganesha) idol was found in one of the mines near Rewati port in 1976.

topPlaces.name: Redi Port
topPlaces.description: Redi Port is a port located at a village Redi in the Konkan coast. Yashwantgad fort and Ganesh temple are the other attractions in this tiny coastal village.

topPlaces.name: Redi, Maharashtra
topPlaces.description: Redi is a village in the district of Sindhudurg in Maharashtra. Originally known as Rewati nagar, Redi is located close to the shores of the Arabian Sea.

topPlaces.name: Fort Tiracol
topPlaces.description: Fort Santíssima Trindade (Fortaleza da Santíssima Trindade in Portuguese) also known as Fort Tiracol, is a Portuguese era fort near the village of Tiracol, in the North Goa district of Goa, India. At the mouth of the Terekhol River, the fort can be reached by a ferry from Querim, 42 km (26 mi) north of Panaji.

topPlaces.name: Tiracol
topPlaces.description: Tiracol is a village on the river right mouth of the Tiracol River and part of the Pernem Sub-Division of North Goa. Due to its location, it is geographically separated from Goa by land making it a Goan exclave in Maharashtra.

topPlaces.name: Paliem, Pernem, Goa
topPlaces.description: Paliem is a village in the Pernem Sub-Division of North Goa, India.

topPlaces.name: Terekhol River
topPlaces.description: The Terekhol or Tiracol River is a river in western India. In its upper reaches it is known as the Banda River and in the lower reaches as the Tiracol.

topPlaces.name: Querim Beach
topPlaces.description: Querim Beach is the sandy coastline of the Querim village of Pernem, Goa. It is the largest and northernmost beach in Goa.

items.note: The Ganapati temple of Redi is located approximately 30 km from Vengurla, in the small village of Redi, Maharashtra, India. The town in which this temple is situated contains iron ore mines and the Ganpati (Ganesha) idol was found in one of the mines near Rewati port in 1976.
Redi Port is a port located at a village Redi in the Konkan coast. Yashwantgad fort and Ganesh temple are the other attractions in this tiny coastal village.
Redi is a village in the district of Sindhudurg in Maharashtra. Originally known as Rewati nagar, Redi is located close to the shores of the Arabian Sea.

Fort Santíssima Trindade (Fortaleza da Santíssima Trindade in Portuguese) also known as Fort Tiracol, is a Portuguese era fort near the village of Tiracol, in the North Goa district of Goa, India. At the mouth of the Terekhol River, the fort can be reached by a ferry from Querim, 42 km (26 mi) north of Panaji.

Tiracol is a village on the river right mouth of the Tiracol River and part of the Pernem Sub-Division of North Goa. Due to its location, it is geographically separated from Goa by land making it a Goan exclave in Maharashtra.
Paliem is a village in the Pernem Sub-Division of North Goa, India.
The Terekhol or Tiracol River is a river in western India. In its upper reaches it is known as the Banda River and in the lower reaches as the Tiracol.
Querim Beach is the sandy coastline of the Querim village of Pernem, Goa. It is the largest and northernmost beach in Goa.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Yashwantgad Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Yashwantgad Fort is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Yashwantgad Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Manohar International Airport, Mopa (North Goa) (21 km) and the nearest railway station is Vasco da Gama (Goa) (43 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Yashwantgad Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Yashwantgad Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Yashwantgad Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Yashwantgad Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Yashwantgad Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Ganapati Temple, Redi, Redi Port, Redi, Maharashtra, Fort Tiracol, Tiracol.
